campbell.title: Campbell City Council — February 3, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to approve a $4,000 donation from Water Tower Kitchen to help local youth with recreation scholarships. The council also discussed plans to build a safer bicycle and pedestrian crossing over Highway 17 at Hamilton Avenue, including reviewing a feasibility study and applying for grant funding to support the project.
keyTopics: $4,000 youth scholarship donation from Water Tower Kitchen
Bicycle and pedestrian bridge over Highway 17 at Hamilton Avenue
Grant application for Hamilton Avenue crossing improvements
Extension of interim ordinance number 2326

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council held a public hearing on March 18, 2026 to get input from residents about a proposed new rule or policy. After hearing from the public, the council moved forward by introducing the ordinance and skipping the normal first-reading step to speed up the process, with plans to vote on final approval at their next meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing conducted to gather resident feedback
New ordinance introduced and fast-tracked for adoption
Vote on final approval scheduled for next regular council meeting
First reading waived to expedite the process

los-altos.title: Los Altos City Council — March 24, 2026
los-altos.summary: The Los Altos City Council met to approve several routine matters including a final report on weed removal efforts around town, new guidelines for how the council conducts its meetings, and a policy allowing certain affordable housing units to change hands more easily. The council also approved raising two flags at City Hall—one for Pride Month and one for Juneteenth—and heard an update from the transit authority.
keyTopics: Weed abatement program completion for 2026
New council meeting procedures and rules adopted
Affordable housing unit transfer policy changes
Progress Pride and Juneteenth flag raising approvals
VTA transit authority presentation

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 7, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council approved several construction and service contracts on April 7, 2026. The main items included hiring QLM, Inc. for construction work, bringing in Dudek for professional services, and joining a statewide insurance program called CJPIA to help manage the city's insurance costs. The council also updated existing agreements with various contractors and discussed an art transfer agreement.
keyTopics: QLM, Inc. construction contract approval
Joining California Joint Powers Insurance Authority (CJPIA) for better insurance rates
Professional services agreement with Dudek
Multiple contract amendments with existing vendors
Art transfer agreement negotiation

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 9, 2026
san-jose.summary: San José's City Council reviewed progress on their plan to improve services for children and youth, including updates on programs designed to keep kids safe over the summer and help at-risk youth. The council also discussed how well the city's library buildings are working and whether they're meeting customer needs, plus received an annual report on code enforcement activities.
keyTopics: Children and Youth Services Master Plan - progress update on citywide youth initiatives
Safe Summer Initiative Grant - keeping kids engaged and safe during summer months
Youth Intervention Services - annual report on programs helping at-risk young people
Library Facilities and Customer Experience - assessment of library condition and user satisfaction
Code Enforcement Annual Report - summary of city violations and enforcement actions

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 7, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council held a meeting on April 7, 2026 that allowed residents to participate both in person and online. Residents could join via Zoom or phone to speak during public comment periods by using the raise hand feature or pressing *9.
keyTopics: Virtual meeting access via Zoom and phone dial-in
Public comment procedures for remote participants
Instructions for residents to speak during council discussion

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 9, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale Human Relations Commission held their April meeting where they honored the flag, heard personal stories from three commissioners about their experiences in Sunnyvale, and reviewed meeting notes from their March gathering. The commission continues its work on community relations and local issues.
keyTopics: Human Relations Commission monthly meeting
Personal stories shared by Commissioners 5, 6, and 7
Approval of March 12, 2026 meeting minutes
Flag salute ceremony

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 8, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council met to discuss three main initiatives: a presentation about Budget and Bites (likely a food/nutrition program), an introduction to Unmuted Tabs, a youth-led organization, and information about summer job opportunities for young people in the city. The council also approved minutes from their previous March meeting and opened the floor for public comments on any city matters.
keyTopics: Budget and Bites program presentation
Unmuted Tabs youth-led organization
City of Cupertino summer jobs for young people
Public comment period on city matters

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 7, 2026
santa-clara.summary: Santa Clara City Council is holding meetings in a hybrid format, meaning residents can attend in person at City Hall or join online via Zoom. The city is making it easy for the public to participate and submit written comments before meetings.
keyTopics: Hybrid meeting format (in-person and remote options available)
Zoom participation details and phone dial-in number provided
How to submit written public comments before meetings using eComment system

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met to approve several administrative and financial matters, including joining a tax board partnership with the county, considering fee waivers for a local educational nonprofit, and awarding contracts for a construction project. Council members also discussed updates to the city's community development plan and reviewed new work requests from individual council members.
keyTopics: Joining Franchise Tax Board partnership with county
Fee waiver request from Milpitas Community Educational organization
Construction contract award and project approval
Updates to 2025-2030 community development plan
New work requests from individual council members

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — March 31, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council reviewed and approved the city's gas utility budget and financial plan for the next fiscal year (2027). The council also approved changes to the rates that residents and businesses pay for natural gas service, with different rate schedules for homes, apartment buildings, and large commercial customers.
keyTopics: Fiscal Year 2027 gas utility budget and financial forecast approved
Natural gas rate changes for residential, multi-unit, and commercial customers
Reserve fund transfers and General Fund transfers for utilities
Meeting minutes from March 4, 2026 Utilities Advisory Commission approved
